sentence_reviews.text: Relational structures encode entities, interactions, molecules, networks, and non-Euclidean domains.
sentence_reviews.evidence_location: PML book1 long TOC: probabilistic graphical models, label propagation on graphs, spectral clustering and graph Laplacian (lines 101-105, 588-593, and 663-670); AIMA contents.html: relational probability models and causal networks (lines 356-392).
sentence_reviews.notes: The sources support graphs and relational/network structures. Molecules, interactions, and non-Euclidean domains are not directly supported by the inspected passages.

sentence_reviews.text: The role of Graphs, hypergraphs & relational data is to connect Graph theory, Scalars, vectors, matrices & tensors to downstream capabilities including Knowledge graphs, ontologies & schemas, Message passing & neighborhood aggregation, Graph neural networks.
sentence_reviews.evidence_location: PML book1 long TOC: graphical models, label propagation on graphs, graph Laplacian spectral clustering, and linear algebra (lines 101-105, 592-593, 663-670, and 206-259); AIMA contents.html: knowledge representation, ontological engineering, relational probability models, and Appendix A.2 linear algebra (lines 333-344, 356-392, and 768-774).
sentence_reviews.notes: Graph/relational data, knowledge representation, and matrix/vector support are present. Hypergraphs, message passing, neighborhood aggregation, and graph neural networks are not directly supported in the inspected locations.

sentence_reviews.text: substrate for Knowledge graphs, ontologies & schemas

sentence_reviews.notes: AIMA directly supports ontologies and knowledge representation. Knowledge graphs and schemas are atlas-level synthesis from the relational/ontology context.

sentence_reviews.text: Encoding choices can discard information, amplify bias, or make important invariances difficult to learn. Its direct dependencies include Graph theory, Scalars, vectors, matrices & tensors.
sentence_reviews.evidence_location: PML book1 long TOC: graph Laplacian/eigenvectors, linear algebra, and graph-based label propagation (lines 206-259, 588-593, and 663-670); AIMA contents.html: fairness/bias and relational/causal graph structures (lines 745-749 and 356-392).
sentence_reviews.notes: Graph and linear-algebra dependencies are supported, and bias is a broad AI concern. Encoding information loss and invariances are not directly supported by the inspected passages.

sentence_reviews.text: Graphs, hypergraphs & relational data rests on the formal or computational capabilities supplied by Graph theory.
sentence_reviews.evidence_location: PML book1 long TOC: graph Laplacian spectral clustering and label propagation on graphs (lines 588-593 and 663-670); AIMA contents.html: causal networks and relational probability models (lines 356-392).

sentence_reviews.text: Graphs, hypergraphs & relational data consumes, emits, or organizes information using Scalars, vectors, matrices & tensors.
sentence_reviews.evidence_location: PML book1 long TOC: linear algebra including matrices, eigenvectors, and graph Laplacian spectral clustering (lines 206-259 and 663-670); AIMA contents.html: Appendix A.2 vectors/matrices/linear algebra (lines 768-774).
sentence_reviews.notes: Matrix/vector representations of graph methods are supported, but the broad relational-data phrasing is synthesized.

edge_reviews.notes: Graph methods, graph Laplacians, label propagation, and relational/causal networks make the requires/core relation defensible.
Graph algorithms commonly use matrices/vectors, and spectral clustering directly uses graph Laplacian eigenvectors. The represents_with/core edge is defensible as a data-representation relation.
